page: Amazon Web Services c8id.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs derived from 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64, supported by 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 15 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in single-threaded and multi-threaded operations. The instance achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core (2205.0), image processing (17608.0), and text processing (2940.0). Multi-threaded decompression via bzip3 reaches 231.29 GB/sec, categorized as strong. In cryptographic and mathematical operations, the hardware utilizes Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), contributing to a strong Passmark CPU score of 51792.67 and an encryption test score of 36211.09.

For machine learning workloads, the CPU's AMX capabilities support small-scale LLM inference, achieving a top-tier text generation speed of 371.41 tokens/second on a 135M parameter model and a strong prompt processing speed of 220.66 tokens/second on a 7B parameter model. However, memory bandwidth for uncached large blocks is limited to 180.41 GB/sec for reads and 82.67 GB/sec for writes, which represents average tier performance. This memory throughput profile, combined with the 2.0 GB per core memory allocation, suggests a tradeoff where memory-bound tasks may face constraints compared to compute-bound operations.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the c8id.8xlarge is characterized by its high compute density and the inclusion of high-speed local NVMe storage, which reduces the need for external block storage attachments for temporary data. This configuration is suitable for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and localized data processing tasks that require high CPU clock speeds and fast local scratch space but do not exceed the 64.0 GB memory boundary.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 32 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory. It features a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD local instance store and a 15 Gbps baseline network interface. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier Geekbench single-core and image processing performance, alongside strong multi-threaded decompression and cryptographic execution supported by AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets. While its 2.0 GB of memory per core presents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications, its high compute density and fast local storage offer qualitative cost efficiency for localized data processing. The c8id.8xlarge is suited for workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and small-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon processor, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.8xlarge is a 32-vCPU compute-optimized instance with 64 GB DDR5 RAM and a 1900 GB NVMe SSD local instance store.
tagline: A compute-optimized platform featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and integrated high-speed NVMe local storage for intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor.
64.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 system memory.
Bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD local instance storage.
Top-tier single-core CPU and image processing benchmark performance.
Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) supporting efficient local LLM inference.
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
